title: quinquina carabayensis
intro: Quinquina carabayensis, also known as Cinchona carabayensis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a tree native to the Andes mountains, traditionally used for its medicinal properties due to the presence of quinine, a compound that has been used to treat malaria and other febrile diseases. The health benefits of Quinquina carabayensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Quinquina carabayensis can be found in traditional medicine, often in the form of tea, infusions, or tinctures, although its modern use is largely limited to historical and cultural contexts.
